Second, to demonstrate the generality of our approach,

of optimizing the 2 hyperparameters of a support vector

concepts.finding:
For the more chal-
lenging CASH problem our MI-SMAC variant of the SMBO
method SMAC (Hutter, Hoos, and Leyton-Brown 2011) (a
state-of-the-art approach for CASH optimization) yielded
substantial improvements, signiﬁcantly outperforming the
previous state of the art for this problem.
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation

concepts.finding:
For a large conﬁguration space describing a CASH problem
in scikit-learn, MI-SMAC substantially improved over the
current state of the art CASH algorithm SMAC (and all other
tested optimizers), showing the potential of our approach
especially for large-scale hyperparameter optimization.
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation

concepts.finding:
The key concept behind meta-learning
for hyperparameter search is to suggest good conﬁgurations
for a novel dataset based on conﬁgurations that are known
to perform well on similar, previously evaluated, datasets.
concepts.implication: Relevant for similarity-based retrieval and lookalike targeting

concepts.finding:

al.

concepts.finding:
We performed a second experiment to test the suitability
of our method for a low-dimensional hyperparameter op-
timization problem: optimizing the complexity penalty C
and the kernel width γ of an SVM using an RBF kernel.
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation

concepts.finding:
Overall, we
found SMAC to outperform both TPE and random search
for this large hyperparameter space, conﬁrming the results

large search space for this problem, which not even SMAC

concepts.finding:
Our
initialization procedure yields mild improvements for low-
dimensional hyperparameter optimization and substantially
improves the state of the art for the more complex combined
algorithm selection and hyperparameter optimization problem.

SMBO requires a substantial number of evaluations to de-
tect high-performance regions when started on a new opti-
mization problem.

most widely studied problem in metalearning, with a large
body of literature and readily available metafeatures and
datasets.2

is spanned by a range of machine learning algorithms from
sciki

concepts.finding:
First, we evaluated the base performance of
the hyperparameter optimization procedures random search,
TPE, and SMAC (note that for TPE the prior distributions
were uniform) on all 57 datasets and then added meta-
learning-initialization to the best of these.
